title: alnus incana var. intermedia
intro: Alnus incana var. intermedia, also known as the narrowleaf mountain alder or intermediate green alder, is a plant species that belongs to the alder family and is characterized by its narrow, lance-shaped leaves and its ability to thrive in wet environments. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial properties. Alnus incana var. intermedia has been reported to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, fighting off infections, and supporting the immune system. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat a range of health issues, including fever, coughs, and respiratory problems.
